description: Pre-Flight Checklist — interactive onboarding readiness tracker for GitHub Copilot. Categories map to airport journey perspectives.
intro: Before every flight, pilots run through a pre-flight checklist. This is yours for GitHub Copilot. Work through each category, check off completed items, and your progress is saved automatically. When every box is green, you're cleared for takeoff.

items.label: GitHub account created and active
items.detail: You need a GitHub account to use any Copilot plan.

items.label: Copilot plan selected (Free, Pro, Pro+, Business, or Enterprise)
items.detail: Free gives 2,000 completions and 50 chat messages/month. Pro and above unlock unlimited completions and agent mode.

items.label: Copilot enabled on your GitHub account or organization
items.detail: For Business/Enterprise, an admin must enable Copilot at the org or enterprise level.

items.label: Billing confirmed (if paid plan)
items.detail: Check Settings → Billing → Copilot to verify your subscription is active.

items.label: Primary IDE installed and up to date
items.detail: VS Code, JetBrains, Visual Studio, Neovim, or Xcode.

items.label: GitHub Copilot extension installed in your IDE
items.detail: Search for 'GitHub Copilot' in your IDE's extension marketplace.

items.label: Signed in to Copilot with your GitHub account
items.detail: The extension will prompt you to authenticate via browser.

items.label: Inline completions appear when you type
items.detail: Open a code file, start typing a function, and verify ghost text appears.

items.label: Used Copilot Chat at least once
items.detail: Open Chat (Ctrl+Shift+I in VS Code) and ask a question about your code.

items.label: Tried @workspace for codebase-wide questions
items.detail: @workspace searches your full project using embeddings. Great for architecture questions.

items.label: Used #file to scope context to a specific file
items.detail: More precise than @workspace when you know which file contains the answer.

items.label: Tried Edit mode (Ctrl+I) for a targeted change
items.detail: Select code, press Ctrl+I, describe the change. Review the diff before accepting.

items.label: Tried Agent Mode for a multi-step task
items.detail: Switch to Agent mode in Chat and ask Copilot to plan and execute a change.

items.label: Reviewed the Copilot threat model
items.detail: Understand data flow, prompt injection risks, and telemetry before using Copilot on sensitive code.

items.label: Understand where your code goes (and doesn't go)
items.detail: Copilot sends context to GitHub's proxy, then to the model provider. No training on your code.

items.label: Content exclusion configured (if Business/Enterprise)
items.detail: Exclude sensitive repos or paths from Copilot completions via org settings.

items.label: Organization Copilot policy reviewed
items.detail: Enable/disable Copilot features per organization. Set suggestion matching to block.

items.label: Seats assigned to the right teams
items.detail: Business/Enterprise: assign Copilot seats via Teams or individual users.

items.label: Audit log streaming configured (Enterprise)
items.detail: Stream Copilot events to your SIEM for compliance monitoring.

items.label: Know which model powers your default completions
items.detail: GPT-4.1 is the default for completions. Chat uses GPT-4.1 or Claude Sonnet depending on the task.

items.label: Tried switching models in Chat (if Pro/Pro+)
items.detail: Pro and above can pick from GPT-5.x, Claude, Gemini, and more via the model picker.

items.label: Understand your premium request budget
items.detail: Free: 0, Pro: 1,000/month, Pro+: 3,000/month. Premium models consume these requests.
